description.value: A heavy wool cloak dyed deep blue, with a silver clasp shaped like a leaf. Well-made, warm, and practical.

unidentified.description: A heavy wool cloak dyed deep blue, with a silver clasp shaped like a leaf. Well-made, warm, and practical.

cursedMeta.decoyAppearance: Cloak of Protection. +1 bonus to AC and saving throws. Requires attunement.
cursedMeta.trueNature: Frost-enchanted cloak unstable near fire. Tracks fire events (fire damage received, fire spells cast within 15 ft). At Phase 2, fire damage triggers additional 1d6 fire damage and nearby fire spells deal 1d4. At Phase 3, escalates to 2d6/1d6. No player whispers. Discovery through unexpected HP loss in combat log.

lure.name: Cloak of Protection
lure.description: You gain a +1 bonus to AC and saving throws while you wear this cloak.

curse.name: Frostmantle
curse.description: The cloak's frost enchantment is unstable near fire. Fire damage destabilises the enchantment, dealing additional fire damage to the wearer. Fire spells cast within 15 ft cause the cloak to spark. The extra damage appears as a sourceless roll in the combat log. No player whispers at any phase.

hints: The silver clasp is cold to the touch even in warm rooms.
Detect Magic shows abjuration with a faint thread of evocation underneath. The two schools are not cooperating cleanly.
The fabric never wrinkles or creases. It hangs perfectly still, as if frozen in shape.

escalation.label: Settle
escalation.effect: The cloak's enchantment flickered when exposed to fire. The player saw nothing. No mechanical effect. No player signal. This phase exists purely for GM awareness.

escalation.label: Fracture
escalation.gmAlertText: Frostmantle reacted to fire. [Actor] took additional fire damage. The player will see this in the combat log as a sourceless roll. You can explain it as residual heat, environmental effect, or say nothing. The cloak cannot be removed.
escalation.effect: The enchantment destabilises near fire. When the wearer takes fire damage: additional 1d6 fire damage (sourceless roll). When a fire spell is cast within 15 ft (not targeting wearer): 1d4 fire damage to the wearer (sourceless roll). No player whisper. The GM is notified each time the cloak reacts so they can manage questions about the extra damage.

escalation.label: Flashpoint
escalation.effect: Full reveal. Fire damage now triggers 2d6 additional. Nearby fire spells trigger 1d6. The frost layer is visibly cracked, glowing orange along the fracture lines. Rename, description update. The mechanic is now explained on the item sheet.

apply.name: Frostmantle
apply.descriptionAppend: Curse: Frostmantle. The cloak's frost enchantment is fractured. Fire destabilises it. When you take fire damage, you take an additional 2d6 fire damage. When a fire spell is cast within 15 ft, you take 1d6 fire damage. The cloak cannot be removed without Remove Curse.

removal.notes: T1. Base Remove Curse (3rd) frees the creature, stops the fire reactions. Over-levelled Remove Curse (5th) purges the item entirely, restoring the frost enchantment.
